name: Iraqi Kahi with Cream
steps: Make a soft dough from the flour and water, and roll into thin layers, brushing with melted butter.
Stack and bake at 400F for 15-18 minutes until golden and flaky.
Serve with the cream and honey.
steps_detailed: Make a soft, elastic dough from the flour, cardamom, and water. Divide into portions and roll each thin.
Brush each layer generously with melted butter, stack, and bake at 400F (200C) for 16-20 minutes until golden and visibly flaky.
Serve with the cream and honey - kahi with geymar (clotted cream) is a beloved Iraqi breakfast, especially on weekends.

name: Iraqi Samoon with Cheese
steps: Warm the bread.
Fill with the cheese and tomatoes.
steps_detailed: Warm the bread (standing in for samoon, Iraq's iconic boat-shaped bread) until soft.
Fill generously with the cheese, tomatoes, and cucumber - a simple, common Iraqi breakfast sandwich.

name: Iraqi Timman Bagilla Style Rice Bowl
steps: Toss the rice with the beans, dill, and butter.
Serve warm.
steps_detailed: Toss the rice with the beans, dill, garlic, and butter until well combined and fragrant.
Serve warm - timman bagilla, dill-scented rice with fava beans, is a beloved Iraqi comfort dish, often eaten for breakfast or alongside fish.

name: Iraqi Dolma
steps: Mix the raw rice with the beef and half the tomatoes.
Stuff into the pepper halves and place in a baking dish with the remaining tomatoes.
Bake at 375F, covered, for 40-45 minutes until the rice is tender.
steps_detailed: Mix the raw rice with the beef, garlic, and dill, and half the tomatoes.
Stuff generously into the pepper halves and arrange in a baking dish, pouring the remaining tomatoes around and over them.
Cover and bake at 375F (190C) for 42-50 minutes until the rice is tender and the peppers are soft.
Dolma, in countless vegetable and leaf variations, is a treasured Iraqi dish, often made as an elaborate mixed-vegetable spread for gatherings.

name: Iraqi Kubba Soup
steps: Soak the bulgur, then mix with a third of the beef and shape into small balls.
Brown the remaining beef with the onion, add the tomatoes and a splash of water, and simmer 20 minutes.
Add the kubba balls and simmer 10-12 more minutes.
steps_detailed: Soak the bulgur in hot water 15 minutes, then drain and mix with a third of the beef, shaping into small, firm balls.
Brown the remaining beef with the onion and garlic, then add the tomatoes, lime juice, and 3/4 cup water, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Gently add the kubba balls and simmer 12-15 more minutes until cooked through.
Kubba, Iraq's beloved bulgur-and-meat dumplings, appear in countless regional soup variations.

name: Iraqi Shorbat Adas
steps: Simmer the lentils with the onion, garlic, and cumin in 1.5 cups water for 20-25 minutes until soft.
Blend until smooth.
Serve with lime and bread.
steps_detailed: Saute the onion and garlic in the oil with the cumin until softened, 5-6 minutes.
Add the lentils and 1.75 cups water, and simmer 22-28 minutes until the lentils are completely soft.
Blend until smooth and finish with the lime juice - shorbat adas, red lentil soup, is a beloved everyday Iraqi lunch.

name: Iraqi Biryani

ingredients.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Brown the chicken with the turmeric.
Add the rice and 1.5 cups water, and simmer, covered, 18-22 minutes until tender.
Fold in the raisins before serving.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Brown the chicken with the onion and turmeric over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the rice and 1.75 cups water, cover, and simmer 20-25 minutes until the rice is tender and fragrant.
Fold in the raisins and top with the walnuts - Iraqi biryani, reflecting deep historical trade ties with South Asia, is a beloved festive dish.

name: Iraqi Masgouf
steps: Rub the fish with the turmeric and garlic.
Grill skin-down over medium heat until cooked through, about 12-15 minutes.
Finish with the lime juice and serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Butterfly the fish and rub thoroughly with the turmeric, garlic, paprika, and oil.
Grill skin-down over medium heat, without flipping, 15-18 minutes until the flesh is cooked through and flaky.
Finish with a generous squeeze of lime and serve with rice - masgouf, traditionally grilled beside an open riverside fire, is widely considered Iraq's national dish.

name: Iraqi Quzi

ingredients.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps: Season the lamb with the turmeric and roast at 350F for 40-45 minutes until tender.
Cook the rice separately and fold in the raisins.
Serve the lamb over the rice.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Raisins, dark, seedless (Includes foods for USDA's Food Distribution Program)

steps_detailed: Season the lamb thoroughly with the turmeric and cardamom, and roast at 350F (175C) for 42-50 minutes until deeply tender.
Cook the rice separately and fold in the raisins.
Serve the lamb over the spiced rice, topped with the walnuts - quzi, whole roasted lamb over richly spiced rice, is Iraq's festive centerpiece dish for celebrations.

name: Iraqi Tashreeb
steps: Simmer the chicken with the onion and tomatoes until tender, about 25-30 minutes.
Tear the bread into a bowl and ladle the chicken and broth over it, letting it soak.
steps_detailed: Simmer the chicken with the onion, garlic, tomatoes, and dill in a splash of water for 28-35 minutes until tender.
Tear the bread into a wide serving bowl and ladle the chicken, vegetables, and broth generously over it, letting the bread soak fully.
Tashreeb, bread soaked in savory broth, is one of Iraq's most ancient and beloved comfort dishes.

name: Iraqi Bamia (Okra and Beef Stew)
steps: Brown the beef with the onion and garlic.
Add the tomatoes and a splash of water, and simmer 20-25 minutes.
Serve with rice.
steps_detailed: Brown the beef with the onion, garlic, and cumin over medium-high heat, 8-10 minutes.
Add the diced tomatoes and 1/2 cup water, and simmer 22-28 minutes until thickened, finishing with the lime juice.
Serve with rice - bamia, a tomato-forward stew traditionally built around okra, is a comforting Iraqi home-cooked staple.

name: Iraqi Kubba Halab (Fried Kubba)
steps: Soak the bulgur, then mix with half the beef and shape into a shell.
Brown the remaining beef with the onion for the filling.
Fill the shells, seal into ovals, and fry until deeply golden.
steps_detailed: Soak the bulgur in hot water 15-20 minutes, then drain and mix with half the beef and the cinnamon, forming a smooth, pliable shell mixture.
Brown the remaining beef with the onion and pistachios for the filling.
Shape the shell mixture around a spoonful of filling into small torpedo shapes, sealing completely.
Fry until deeply golden on all sides, 4-6 minutes - kubba, in its many shapes and cooking methods, is Iraq's most iconic snack food.

name: Iraqi Hummus

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, and garlic with a splash of water until smooth.
Serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids
Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil with a splash of water until very smooth.
Serve with warm bread - hummus is common mezze fare across Iraq.

name: Iraqi Baba Ghanoush

ingredients.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps: Roast the whole eggplant until completely soft and collapsed.
Scoop out the flesh and mash with the tahini, lime juice, and garlic.
Serve with flatbread.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Seeds, sesame butter, tahini, from roasted and toasted kernels (most common type)

steps_detailed: Roast the whole eggplant at 425F (220C) for 28-35 minutes, turning occasionally, until charred and completely soft.
Scoop out the flesh and mash with the tahini, lime juice, garlic, and oil.
Serve with warm bread - a smoky, beloved Iraqi mezze dish.

name: Iraqi Falafel

ingredients.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, and cilantro into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls and fry until deeply golden, about 3-4 minutes per side.

ingredients_full.ingredient: Chickpeas (garbanzo beans, bengal gram), mature seeds, canned, drained solids

steps_detailed: Blend the chickpeas, garlic, cilantro, flour, and cumin in a food processor into a coarse, moldable paste.
Shape into small balls or patties.
Fry in the oil over medium heat, 3-5 minutes per side, until deeply golden and crisp - falafel is popular street food across Iraq.

name: Iraqi Kleicha
steps: Mix the flour, butter, and sugar into a soft, crumbly dough.
Flatten portions, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape into small rounds.
Bake at 350F for 18-22 minutes until just set.
steps_detailed: Mix the flour, butter, sugar, and cardamom into a soft, slightly crumbly dough.
Flatten a portion, fill with the mashed dates, and reshape around it, sealing completely, often pressed with a decorative mold or fork pattern.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 18-24 minutes until just set and very pale - kleicha is considered Iraq's national cookie, especially treasured during Eid.

name: Iraqi Zerda
steps: Simmer the rice in 1.5 cups water with the sugar, saffron, and butter for 20-25 minutes until tender and golden.
Serve warm.
steps_detailed: Simmer the rice in 1.75 cups water with the sugar, saffron, and butter over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, 22-28 minutes until tender and richly golden.
Top with the chopped pistachios and serve warm - zerda, saffron-sweetened rice, is a treasured Iraqi dessert for celebrations.

name: Iraqi Baklava
steps: Layer the phyllo with the melted butter, scattering the chopped walnuts between layers.
Bake at 350F for 25-30 minutes until deeply golden.
Pour the honey over while still warm.
steps_detailed: Layer sheets of phyllo in a small buttered dish, brushing each with melted butter, scattering the walnuts mixed with cardamom between layers.
Cut into diamond shapes before baking.
Bake at 350F (175C) for 28-35 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Pour the honey evenly over the hot baklava as soon as it comes out of the oven - baklava has deep roots in Iraqi and wider Mesopotamian dessert tradition.

name: Iraqi Amba
steps: Mash the mango with the turmeric and lime juice.
Season with salt and let sit 10 minutes before serving.
steps_detailed: Mash the mango with the turmeric, lime juice, and garlic until mostly smooth with some texture.
Amba, a tangy pickled mango condiment, originated in Iraq's Jewish community and remains a beloved sauce for sandwiches and grilled meats.

name: Iraqi Dagga
steps: Mash the tomatoes with the garlic, jalapeno, and lime juice.
Season with salt and serve alongside grilled meats.
steps_detailed: Mash the tomatoes with the garlic, jalapeno, lime juice, and cilantro into a rough, fresh relish.
Dagga, a simple fresh tomato relish, is a common Iraqi table condiment.
